title: Prototype record: PM item timeline and stage snapshot
objective: Test whether PMs can inspect one item timeline and a discovery-stage aggregate without opening multiple disconnected artifacts first.
uncertainty_statement: It is still unclear whether a high-detail interactive lifecycle view can feel trustworthy enough to support real PM review and handoff decisions.
primary_test_scenario: A PM opens the item timeline, checks gate evidence, and decides whether the item is ready for PRD handoff.
experience_quality_target: The prototype should feel detailed and credible enough for a realistic workflow walkthrough instead of a narrated mockup.
tested_assumptions: An item-first lifecycle surface reduces manual reconstruction during PM review
Visible gate outcomes and linked artifacts improve trust in the current item state
proof_generated: PMs want the current stage, gate outcome, and linked artifacts visible together
A stage aggregate only works when it links back to concrete item ids
quality_reference_examples: A multi-pane workflow surface that supports real handoff review without presenter narration
learnings: PMs expect the current stage, gate outcome, and linked artifacts to be visible without extra navigation.
A stage aggregate is useful only when it can still link back to concrete item ids.
The PRD handoff feels believable when validation evidence is visible beside the timeline.
improvement_actions: Increase interaction density around evidence previews so the PM can inspect without leaving the flow.
Strengthen visual distinction between current stage, passed gates, and deferred downstream work.
strategic_implication: The prototype supports a challenger posture centered on lifecycle explanation and trust rather than broad workflow breadth.
